get.description: Get a list of installed extensions.

parameters.description: JSON filter expression specifying the items to return
parameters.name: filter

parameters.description: Sort ordering for returned items (default +name)
parameters.name: sort

parameters.description: Zero based offset to start from
parameters.name: offset

parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return (1 – 1000)
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Whether to calculate the total item count
parameters.name: calculate_count

get.summary: Get a list of installed extensions

post.description:
Installs an extension from the extension store.

The query parameter sync=true may be provided to make this a synchronous operation. In this case, the operation produces JSON status updates during the operation using HTTP chunked transfer encoding.

The default is asynchronous operation. To check on the progress of the operation, use GET /extension/instance/{id}.

parameters.description: ID of the extension instance
parameters.name: id

parameters.description: True to send a kill signal to the extension before deleting
parameters.name: force

get.description: Get details of an installed extension

parameters.description: ID of the extension instance
parameters.name: id

get.summary: Get details of an installed extension

patch.description: Change the state of an installed extension

parameters.description: ID of the extension instance
parameters.name: id

patch.summary: Change the state of an installed extension

get.description:
Get the configuration of an installed extension.

Extensions that support configuration are marked with the has_config: true property.

An extension’s configuration may be an arbitrary JSON object. Check the documentation for the extension to determine the settings that may be configured.

parameters.description: ID of the extension instance
parameters.name: id

get.summary: Get the configuration of an installed extension

put.description: Set the configuration of an installed extension

parameters.description: ID of the extension instance
parameters.name: id

put.summary: Set the configuration of an installed extension

get.description:
Get the log output from an installed extension.

Using tail=all may cause browser issues if the extension has a large amount of logs.

parameters.description: ID of the extension instance
parameters.name: id

parameters.description: Include extension’s standard-output messages
parameters.name: stdout

parameters.description: Include extension’s standard-error messages
parameters.name: stderr

parameters.description: Specify a UNIX timestamp to only return log entries since that time
parameters.name: since

parameters.description: Prefix every log line with its UTC timestamp
parameters.name: timestamps

parameters.description: Return this number of lines at the end of the logs, or "all" for everything
parameters.name: tail

get.summary: Get the log output from an installed extension

post.description:
Reinstalls an extension instance.

The reinstall process will recreate the extension using the same settings it currently has.

This operation will download the latest updates for this version of the extension from the extension store.

The body parameters state, files and/or ip_address, if non-null, can be used to change the current settings of the extension. If these parameters are not supplied or set to null the existing settings will be used.

The query parameter sync=true may be provided to make this a synchronous operation. In this case, the operation produces JSON status updates during the operation using HTTP chunked transfer encoding.

The default is asynchronous operation. To check on the progress of the operation, use GET /extension/instance/{id}. The install_time property will be updated when the extension is reinstalled.

parameters.description: ID of the extension instance
parameters.name: id

parameters.description: ID of the extension instance
parameters.name: id

parameters.description: ID of the extension instance
parameters.name: id

parameters.description: ID of the extension instance
parameters.name: id

get.description:
Get information on the available upgrades for an extension.

If there is an upgrade, the details of the latest version in the store will be returned, as well if the upgrade will be a major or minor one.

Will return "up to date" if no upgrade is available"

parameters.description: ID of the extension instance
parameters.name: id

get.summary: Get information on an extension's available upgrade

post.description:
Install any available upgrades for an extension.

If it is a minor upgrade, this process will recreate the extension using the same settings it currently has.

This operation will download the latest updates for this version of the extension from the extension store.

The body parameters state, files and/or ip_address, if non-null, can be used to change the current settings of the extension. If these parameters are not supplied or set to null the existing settings will be used.

The query parameter sync=true may be provided to make this a synchronous operation. In this case, the operation produces JSON status updates during the operation using HTTP chunked transfer encoding.

The default is asynchronous operation. To check on the progress of the operation, use GET /extension/instance/{id}. The install_time property will be updated when the extension is upgraded.

parameters.description: ID of the extension instance
parameters.name: id
